description: Fix false-positive authentication detection where isAuthenticated returns true for anonymous visitors because the csrftoken cookie exists for all users.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/pinterest && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add login-specific check to isAuthenticated
userStories.description: isAuthenticated only checks for csrftoken cookie, which Pinterest sets for all visitors. Add a check for a login-specific signal.
acceptanceCriteria: isAuthenticated returns false for anonymous/logged-out users
isAuthenticated returns true for logged-in users
The check uses a signal that is reliably present only for authenticated users (e.g., a user-specific cookie or page global)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/pinterest/src/pinterest-api.ts (fix isAuthenticated)

Context:
The current auth check (line 8) only checks for the csrftoken cookie:
```typescript
export const isAuthenticated = (): boolean => getCsrfToken() !== null;
```

Pinterest sets the `csrftoken` cookie for ALL visitors, including anonymous ones. This means the plugin reports as ready even when the user is logged out, and all tool calls then fail with auth errors.

Pinterest sets additional cookies/state for logged-in users. Options:
1. Check for a `_auth` or `_pinterest_sess` cookie that is only set for logged-in users
2. Check for a user-specific page global (e.g., `window.__NEXT_DATA__` with user data, or `window.isAuth`)
3. Check for a specific DOM element that only appears for logged-in users

Read the pinterest-api.ts file to understand what auth data the plugin extracts for API calls (Bearer token, user ID, etc.). The isAuthenticated check should verify that the actual auth data needed for API calls is available, not just the CSRF token.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the CSRF token extraction function
- The fix should be reliable across Pinterest page loads (not dependent on DOM timing)
